inline_comments.message:
I had kind of hoped to keep any knowledge of Policy Violations out of Gizmo.  

My thought was the Gizmo should remain unaware of who was consuming the event requests downstream, and that if Sentinel or Champ or some other service that has been inserted into the flow wants to fail a request, they would populate the 'result' and 'error-message' fields of the GraphEvent response object with appropriate values, and Gizmo would send a corresponding error response to the originating client.

IMO Gizmo shouldn't care whether it is Sentinel failing the write because of a policy violation, or whether Champ is failing the write because of a Database Index uniqueness violation.  

Is there a reason you want Gizmo to be aware of policy violations?  My preference is that if Sentinel wants to fail a write request, that Sentinel would put the information about the violated policy in the errorMessage field, and that Gizmo would treat that just like an error it got from Champ or some other service.
